rulesStrings.li: overview->Recorded by [artist], this [format] blends [genre] with [genre2] influences
overview->The liner notes credit [artist] as the sole composer and performer of this [genre] [format]
overview->A live [genre] [format] captured somewhere in [worldname], credited to [artist]
overview->A collaborative [genre] [format] from the [worldname] music scene, featuring [artist]
overview->Somewhere between [genre] and [genre2], this [format] by [artist] resists easy classification
overview->Pure [genre] with no concessions and no crossover. [artist] at their most committed
overview->A [genre] [format] by [artist] that defined what the genre could sound like in its era
production->The liner notes say it was pressed in limited quantities by a small label on [worldname]
production->According to the [disc_location], it was self-released by [artist] after a string of rejections from every [genre] label they approached
production->The [disc_location] says the [genre] recording was made in a single overnight session
production->The liner notes indicate this was the last [genre] work [artist] ever recorded
production->A stamped note on the [disc_location] says this was one of only fifty copies pressed
production->The [disc_location] notes that all master recordings were lost in a fire, making this one of the few surviving documents of their [genre] period
production->According to the [disc_location], the original pressing sold poorly but the [genre] community has since reconsidered it
production->The [disc_location] claims the [genre] sessions were recorded in a single room with no professional equipment
production->A handwritten note inside says copies were distributed free of charge at [genre] shows across [worldname]
production->The [disc_location] credits over a dozen [genre] musicians, none of whom are named anywhere else
production->The [disc_location] says [artist] spent three years writing these [genre] compositions before recording them
production->According to the [disc_location], this [genre] [format] was intended for a film that was never made
reception(quality[greater_than]4)->By most accounts it is considered a landmark [genre] recording of its era
reception(quality[greater_than]4)->The [disc_location] is filled with glowing critical praise for its contribution to [genre]
reception(quality[greater_than]4)->Those who care about [genre] tend to speak of it with unusual reverence
reception(quality[greater_than]3)->It found an appreciative audience among [genre] listeners, if not a large one
reception(quality[greater_than]3)->Most who encounter it seem to find something in the [genre] that moves them
reception(quality[less_than]4)->Critics of the [genre] were divided, though general listeners seemed warmer to it
reception(quality[less_than]4)->It passed largely without notice on release, even within [genre] circles
reception(quality[less_than]3)->Reviews at the time were unkind, calling the [genre] approach derivative
reception(quality[less_than]2)->It is unclear why it was ever pressed at all
reception(quality==6)->Those who know it speak of it in hushed tones, calling it a quiet landmark of the [genre] tradition
anecdote->According to the [disc_location], [artist] wrote the opening [genre] track during a siege
anecdote->A note inside says that [artist] refused to listen to a playback of the finished [genre] recording
anecdote->The [disc_location] mentions that [artist] was reportedly in poor health throughout the [genre] sessions
anecdote->According to the [disc_location], [artist] dedicated the [format] to someone named only as "R"
anecdote->The [disc_location] claims [artist] was unaware the [genre] sessions were being recorded
anecdote->A scrawled note on the inner sleeve reads "this one is for those who didn't make it"
anecdote->According to the [disc_location], the [genre] [format] was recorded in an abandoned building on [worldname]
anecdote->The [disc_location] says [artist] performed the entire [genre] [format] in one continuous take
anecdote->According to the [disc_location], [artist] never spoke publicly about this [format] or what drove them toward [genre] at that point in their life
anecdote->According to the [disc_location], [artist] considered this [genre] [format] a failure and was reportedly furious it was released
anecdote->The [disc_location] describes a [genre] scene that no longer exists, captured here by accident
anecdote->A note inside says [artist] had never worked in [genre] before this recording and never returned to it afterward
anecdote->The [disc_location] suggests [artist] made this [format] as a deliberate departure from their earlier [genre2] work
quality_statement(quality[greater_than]4)->Listening to it, it is easy to understand why some consider it essential [genre]
quality_statement(quality[greater_than]4)->There is something in the [genre] here that feels genuinely irreplaceable
quality_statement(quality[greater_than]4)->For anyone with an interest in [genre], it is required listening
quality_statement(quality[greater_than]3)->It holds up well as a [genre] document, even across the distance of years
quality_statement(quality[greater_than]3)->Not everyone would call it great [genre], but most would call it good
quality_statement(quality[less_than]4)->Its approach to [genre] has flaws, but the effort behind it is audible
quality_statement(quality[less_than]3)->It is more interesting as an artifact of the [genre] scene than as a listening experience
quality_statement(quality[less_than]2)->It is hard to recommend, even to completionists of the [genre]
